rationale: The citing paper uses Nunes and Park (2003) as core prior evidence that nonmonetary promotion formats reduce consumers' tendency to compare gift value with focal product price because values are harder to translate into a common unit. This prior mechanism is used in the theoretical development to motivate the paper's own prediction that voucher formats similarly dampen comparison and value sensitivity. The citation is therefore part of hypothesis framing and mechanism positioning, not just a background list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Nunes and Park's incommensurability logic from gift-versus-discount comparisons to voucher-versus-direct-gift conditional promotions. It builds on the idea that harder cross-unit comparison lowers sensitivity to promotion value and tests this in a new promotional format setting.
